name: Østfold (Norway, 1958–2019)

colors.symbolism: the field of the county arms

description: A red field charged with a stylised gold rocky coastal outline running across the field.
history: Granted to Ostfold county on 23 May 1958. The county was abolished on 1 January 2020 when it was merged with Akershus and Buskerud into the new Viken county; following Viken's dissolution, Ostfold was re-established on 1 January 2024 with new arms and flag.

features.symbolism: heraldic banner of the county arms: a gold (Or) outline of the rocky coastal landscape of Ostfold on a red field, suggesting the bedrock and skerries of the region
the characteristic skerried coast and bedrock of Ostfold
